file.content:
# WP-050 — LNS-V3-01: Lens dropdown in status bar

**Branch:** `feat/v3-lns-v3-01-lens-dropdown`
**Effort:** S — 4 days
**Status:** `now` (depends on DSC-V3-02 / WP-049 — merge order: DSC-V3-02 merges first)

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §8 T8 LNS-V3-01 entry; §3 Design Pillars D8; anti-pattern A8; §A Agent Instructions
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/StatusBar.tsx` — existing status bar; add the lens chip here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — `LensMode` type (added by WP-049 / DSC-V3-02; if not present, add a stub)

**Note on dependency.** This WP depends on `SetViewLensCmd` and `LensMode` from DSC-V3-02 (WP-049). Both WPs run as concurrent agents. If those types are not yet in main when you branch, add minimal stubs locally — the orchestrator will resolve conflicts when merging DSC-V3-02 first, then LNS-V3-01.

---

## Verbatim LNS-V3-01 tracker entry

#### LNS-V3-01 — Lens dropdown in status bar

**Status.** `now`. Antidote to A8.
**Scope.** Status-bar dropdown "Show: ▾" — 4-item menu: Architecture / Structure / MEP / All. Click flips `defaultLens` on the active view only. Foreground/ghost transition runs `--ease-paper` over 240 ms. Keyboard `L` cycles forward.
**Data model.** No new fields; reuses `defaultLens` from DSC-V3-02.
**Engine.** `SetViewLens(activeViewId, lensMode)` — undoable, activity-stream-aware.
**UI.** Right side of status bar, left of zoom indicator. Chip reads "Show: Architecture ▾." Check-mark on active. When lens ≠ `show_all`, chip's left edge tints `--disc-*-soft`.
**Acceptance.** Click "Show: ▾" → "Structure." In 240 ms arch walls fade to 25% ghost. Click "All" → everything returns. Two clicks vs Revit's 120-toggle dialog.
**Effort.** S — 4 days.

---

## Setup

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
git fetch origin
git checkout main
git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-lns-v3-01-lens-dropdown
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. Stub types (if DSC-V3-02 not yet in main)

If `LensMode` is not exported from `packages/core/src/index.ts`, add locally:

```ts
export type LensMode = 'show_arch' | 'show_struct' | 'show_mep' | 'show_all';
export type SetViewLensCmd = { type: 'set_view_lens'; viewId: string; lens: LensMode };
```

These will be deduped when the orchestrator merges DSC-V3-02 first.

### 2. `LensDropdown` component (`packages/web/src/workspace/LensDropdown.tsx`)

```tsx
const LENS_LABELS: Record<LensMode, string> = {
  show_arch: 'Architecture',
  show_struct: 'Structure',
  show_mep: 'MEP',
  show_all: 'All',
};

const LENS_CYCLE: LensMode[] = ['show_arch', 'show_struct', 'show_mep', 'show_all'];

export function LensDropdown({ viewId, currentLens, onChangeLens }: {
  viewId: string;
  currentLens: LensMode;
  onChangeLens: (lens: LensMode) => void;
}) { ... }
```

- Renders a chip: `Show: {LENS_LABELS[currentLens]} ▾`
- When `currentLens !== 'show_all'`, left border tints with `var(--disc-arch-soft)` / `var(--disc-struct-soft)` / `var(--disc-mep-soft)` as appropriate
- Click opens a 4-row menu (using existing dropdown primitive — no custom portal unless one exists)
- Each row shows the lens label + a check-mark SVG glyph when active
- Select row → calls `onChangeLens(lens)` + closes menu
- Menu closes on outside click or `Esc`
- `transition: opacity 240ms var(--ease-paper)` on the ghost elements is handled by the renderer (DSC-V3-02); this component only dispatches the command
- **No hex literals.** Every colour via `var(--*)` token.

### 3. Keyboard shortcut `L` (`packages/web/src/workspace/LensDropdown.tsx` or global keymap)

Wire `L` key (when no input is focused and no draw tool is active) to cycle through `LENS_CYCLE` on the active view:

```ts
const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
  if (e.key === 'l' || e.key === 'L') {
    if (document.activeElement?.tagName === 'INPUT') return;
    const idx = LENS_CYCLE.indexOf(currentLens);
    onChangeLens(LENS_CYCLE[(idx + 1) % LENS_CYCLE.length]);
  }
};
```

Register via `useEffect` on `window`. Deregister on unmount.

### 4. Wire into `StatusBar.tsx`

In `packages/web/src/workspace/StatusBar.tsx`, import `LensDropdown` and render it in the right slot (left of zoom indicator):

```tsx
<LensDropdown
  viewId={activeViewId}
  currentLens={activeView?.defaultLens ?? 'show_all'}
  onChangeLens={(lens) => dispatch({ type: 'set_view_lens', viewId: activeViewId, lens })}
/>
```

`dispatch` calls the existing command bus / store action → `POST /api/v3/models/{id}/apply` with `SetViewLensCmd`.

### 5. Activity stream entry

`SetViewLensCmd` dispatches an activity entry `{ kind: 'lens_changed', viewId, lens }` via the existing activity bus. No new activity type needed if a generic `view_updated` entry already exists; otherwise add a minimal `LensChangedEntry` to `activity.py`.

### 6. Tests

No Python tests needed (this is frontend-only; the engine side is tested in WP-049).

Add a Playwright or React Testing Library test in `packages/web/src/workspace/__tests__/LensDropdown.test.tsx`:

- Renders "Show: All ▾" initially
- Click opens the 4-item menu
- Click "Structure" calls `onChangeLens('show_struct')`
- `L` key cycles forward through the 4 modes

---

## Implementation rules

- **No scope creep.** Do not add LNS-V3-02 (discipline chrome tints) here.
- **Frontend only.** The ghost rendering pass is owned by DSC-V3-02. This WP only owns the status-bar UI + command dispatch.
- **Design pillar D8.** One dropdown, per-view, no global matrix, no dialog.
- **No hex literals** in any `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`.

---

## Verify gate

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
make verify
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
# Run frontend unit test if available:
# pnpm --filter web test -- LensDropdown
```

---

## Commit and push

```bash
git add packages/web/src/workspace/LensDropdown.tsx \
        packages/web/src/workspace/StatusBar.tsx \
        packages/core/src/index.ts
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(lns): LNS-V3-01 lens dropdown in status bar

LensDropdown chip in status bar right slot; L key cycles forward; Show: {mode}
chip with disc-soft left tint when lens engaged; dispatches SetViewLensCmd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git push -u origin feat/v3-lns-v3-01-lens-dropdown
```

Do NOT open a PR and do NOT merge to main.

**Orchestrator note:** Merge WP-049 (DSC-V3-02) before this WP to avoid conflicts on `LensMode` / `SetViewLensCmd` definitions.

---

## Final report template

```
WP: LNS-V3-01
Prompt file: spec/v3-prompts/wp-050.md
Branch: feat/v3-lns-v3-01-lens-dropdown
Final commit: <full SHA>
Verify: green | red — <reason if red>
Acceptance: yes | partial — <gap if partial> | no
Files touched: <count>
Lines: +<add> -<del>
As-shipped notes: <2-3 sentences>
Follow-up surfaced: <list or none>
```

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
